findings.title: Whitelist free mint can be stolen by passing an arbitrary whitelisted address

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can steal another user's whitelist mint by supplying an arbitrary `_user`

findings.title: Contract callers can revert losing mints and keep only winning outcomes

best_agent_match.title: A wrapper contract can revert every losing mint and keep only winning outcomes

findings.title: Prize randomness is block-level and predictable enough for builders to cherry-pick winning blocks

best_agent_match.title: Prize randomness is derived from block fields that are predictable, proposer-influenceable, and constant for the whole block

findings.title: Hardcoded `send` recipient can permanently brick winner payouts and withdrawals

best_agent_match.title: Immutable `send`-based payout target can permanently brick withdrawals and winning mints

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy and Silent Failure in ETH Transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: `send()` results are checked with `require`, so failures revert the whole transaction rather than silently leaving inconsistent state. The claimed reentrancy angle is not supported because `send()` forwards only 2300 gas and `_safeMint` also has an explicit reentrancy guard around minting.

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect Price Calculation in freeMint
rejected_candidates.reason: The lucky payout in `freeMint` appears to be the intended game mechanic, not a code defect that causes protocol-level harm by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on addBonusPool Function
rejected_candidates.reason: A public payable top-up function is not a vulnerability on its own; allowing anyone to donate ETH to the bonus pool does not create a realistic exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Unused withdrawAddress Update Functionality
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of a setter is not independently reportable as an exploit. The only plausible harm is the separate `send()`-based bricking issue already captured in F-004.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Event Emission in Critical Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing events may hinder off-chain monitoring but do not create realistic protocol-level harm such as theft, insolvency, lockup, or permissionless DoS.
